title: CITE-seq for PBMCs

steps: Warm up 10x the volume of thawing medium (RPMI + 10% FBS).
Thaw frozen PBMCs in 37 °C, water bath .
Transfer sample into a 15 ml tube.
Add 10x volume of thawing medium dropwise into tube.
Spray down with 70% EtOH.
Centrifuge 500 rcf, °C, 5 minutes .
Remove the sealed bucket from the centrifuge and place into the Class II MSC.
Discard supernatant.
Resuspend in 1 mL, RBC lysis buffer for 00:03:00 to remove erytrocytes.
Resuspend the cell pellet in 500 µL, FACS buffer . Equal volume for all patients.
Perform ficoll to eliminate dead cells.
Recover live cells.
Count cells using trypan blue. One fraction of these PBMCs will be used directly and another fraction will be used for CD3+ cells depletion.
Add 800 µL, Ficoll and 500 µL, cell suspension to a centrifuge conical tube.
Centrifuge at 10000 rcf, °C, 10 seconds .
Swap the tube and centrifuge again at 10000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds .
Wash with 1 mL, FACS buffer .
Centrifuge and discard supernatant.
Resuspend in 250 mL, FACS buffer .
With the first fraction of PBMCs: pool 125,000 of live PBMCs from each patient (2 patients) in low binding tubes; 250,000 cells in total. IMPORTANT: Keep the cells On ice all times!!
Cells should be pooled in a final volume of exactly 100 µL .
Proceed with CD3 negative cells isolation using the second fraction of PBMCs:
Centrifuge and remove 87.5 µL, supernatant . Final volume 12.5 µL .
Use 2x10 6 of the PBMCs ( 200 µL, cell suspension + 20 µL, anti-CD3 magnetic beads ).
Resuspend in 1 mL, FACS buffer .
Pass the cells through a MS magnetic column with 3 washes of 500 µL .
Centrifuge the eluted fraction and resuspend in 200 µL, FACS buffer .
Count CD3 negative cells using trypan blue.
Pool 125,000 of CD3 negative cells from each patient (2 patients) in low binding tubes = 250,000 cells in total IMPORTANT: Keep the cells on ice at all times!!
Cells should be pooled in a final volume of exactly 100 µL .
Incubate 00:15:00 at 4 °C (not ice).
Centrifuge and remove supernatant.
Resuspend in 500 µL, FACS buffer .
Centrifuge and remove 87.5 µL, supernatant . Final volume 12.5 µL .
Prepare CITE-seq Antibody mix:
Use one aliquot of AB pool per sample; centrifuge at 14000 rcf, 4 °C, 1 minute .
Add 50 µL, FACS buffer to reconstitute.
Vortex.
Incubate in the dark for 00:05:00 to ensure the antibodies are completely resuspended.
Centrifuge 14000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes .
During the centrifugation, add 1.25 µL, Human TruStain FcX™ Fc Blocking Reagent per tube.
Incubate 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Add 12.5 µL, CITE-seq AB mix to FC blocked samples, taking care to avoid any pellet (if one exists).
Incubate for 00:30:00 at 4 °C in dark.
Add 1 mL, FACS buffer .
Place tubes in centrifuge bucket inside the Class II MSC and close the lid.
Centrifuge the samples at 500 rcf, 4 °C, 5 minutes (swing bucket centrifuge).
Discard the supernatant.
Repeat wash steps two more times (total of 3 washes). go to step #31
Resuspend cells in 400 µL, 1x PBS + 0.04 % BSA .
Filter cells through 40 μm strainers (Flowmi Cell Strainer).
Pass an extra volume of 400 µL through the same flowmi strainer to recover the remaining cells.
Centrifuge the sample at 500 rcf, 4 °C, 5 minutes and remove 700 µL, supernatant . Final volume will be around 100 µL .
Count cells using Trypan blue.

Proceed immediately to the 10x Genomics Single Cell protocol. NB: The aim to is collect a total of ~7,000 cells per patient. For this, 25,000 cells of the pool of 2 samples will be loaded into one channel of the 10x Chromium.
Take 5 µL, cell suspension + 10 µL, Trypan blue (final dilution 1:3).
Add 9 ml, FACs buffer .
Centrifuge at 400 rcf, °C, 5 minutes .
Remove supernatant.
creator: Alaina Spivey
